Operation 'delete-/cms/v3/pages/site-pages/{objectId}_archive' has semantic quality issues: missing_side_effects, missing_output_details. The current description lacks important context about the permanence of the deletion and what response to expect. Added clarification that deletion is permanent and cannot be undone, plus explicit mention of the expected HTTP response for better AI agent understanding of operation completion.

data.current_summary: Delete a Site Page
data.current_description: Delete the Site Page object identified by the id in the path.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes the Site Page object identified by the objectId in the path. This operation cannot be undone. Returns a 204 No Content response on successful deletion.
data.reasoning: The current description lacks important context about the permanence of the deletion and what response to expect. Added clarification that deletion is permanent and cannot be undone, plus explicit mention of the expected HTTP response for better AI agent understanding of operation completion.

message: Operation 'post-/cms/v3/pages/landing-pages/folders/batch/read_readFolders' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, not_actionable, missing_output_details. The current description incorrectly states 'Update' when the operation is clearly a read/retrieve operation based on the path and summary. The summary is vague about what is being retrieved. Improved version clarifies the batch read operation, specifies the input format (array of IDs), explains the return value, and documents the 207 response behavior for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Retrieve a batch of Folders
data.current_description: Update the Folder objects identified in the request body.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve multiple content folders by ID
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves multiple ContentFolder objects in a batch operation. Accepts an array of folder IDs in the request body and returns the corresponding folder objects. Supports partial success with a 207 Multi-Status response when some folders are not found or inaccessible.
data.reasoning: The current description incorrectly states 'Update' when the operation is clearly a read/retrieve operation based on the path and summary. The summary is vague about what is being retrieved. Improved version clarifies the batch read operation, specifies the input format (array of IDs), explains the return value, and documents the 207 response behavior for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'post-/cms/v3/pages/site-pages/ab-test/create-variation_createABTestVariation' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description lacks clarity about what is returned and the side effect of creating a new variation. Added information about the return value (newly created variation object), HTTP status code, and clarified the relationship to the parent A/B test for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Create a new A/B test variation
data.current_description: Create a new A/B test variation based on the information provided in the request body.
data.description_suggestion: Create a new A/B test variation for a site page based on the configuration provided in the request body. The variation will be created with the specified settings and associated with the parent A/B test. Returns the newly created variation object with a 201 status code.
data.reasoning: The current description lacks clarity about what is returned and the side effect of creating a new variation. Added information about the return value (newly created variation object), HTTP status code, and clarified the relationship to the parent A/B test for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Update languages of multi-language group
data.current_description: Explicitly set new languages for each site page in a multi-language group.
data.description_suggestion: Explicitly set new languages for each site page in a multi-language group. This operation replaces the existing language configuration with the languages specified in the request body. Returns the updated multi-language group configuration. All site pages in the group will be updated to reflect the new language settings.
data.reasoning: Missing details about what is returned and the side effects of updating languages. Added information about the replacement behavior, return value, and impact on all pages in the group to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'post-/cms/v3/pages/landing-pages/ab-test/rerun_rerunPreviousABTest' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description is too minimal and lacks context. Added information about what 'rerun' means (restart with prior configuration), what is returned, and the side effect of beginning new data collection to help AI agents understand the operation's purpose and effects.

data.current_summary: Rerun a previous A/B test
data.current_description: Rerun a previous A/B test.
data.description_suggestion: Rerun a previous A/B test for a landing page using the configuration from a prior test. This operation restarts the A/B test with the specified variations and settings. Returns the restarted A/B test object with updated status and timing information. The test will begin collecting new performance data.
data.reasoning: Description is too minimal and lacks context. Added information about what 'rerun' means (restart with prior configuration), what is returned, and the side effect of beginning new data collection to help AI agents understand the operation's purpose and effects.

message: Operation 'post-/cms/v3/pages/landing-pages/folders/batch/archive_archiveFolders' has semantic quality issues: inconsistent_terminology, missing_output_details. Summary uses 'Delete' but description says 'Delete' which is inconsistent with the endpoint path '/archive'. Changed to 'Archive' for consistency with API semantics. Added clarification about what archiving means and what is returned in the batch response.

data.current_summary: Archive a batch of Folders
data.current_description: Archive the Folder objects identified in the request body.
data.description_suggestion: Archive the Folder objects identified in the request body. Archiving removes folders from active use while preserving their data. Returns a batch response indicating the success or failure status for each folder archive operation.
data.reasoning: Summary uses 'Delete' but description says 'Delete' which is inconsistent with the endpoint path '/archive'. Changed to 'Archive' for consistency with API semantics. Added clarification about what archiving means and what is returned in the batch response.

message: Operation 'post-/cms/v3/pages/site-pages/batch/create_createBatch' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Missing information about return values and batch operation behavior. Added details about what is returned (newly created pages with IDs), the partial success capability (207 response code indicates this), and clarified that this is a batch operation for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Create a batch of Site Pages
data.current_description: Create the Site Page objects detailed in the request body.
data.description_suggestion: Create multiple Site Page objects in a single batch operation based on the page configurations provided in the request body. Returns a batch response containing the newly created page objects with their assigned IDs. Supports partial success where some pages may succeed while others fail.
data.reasoning: Missing information about return values and batch operation behavior. data.current_summary: Restore a previous version of a Site Page
data.current_description: Takes a specified version of a Site Page and restores it.
data.description_suggestion: Restores a Site Page to a specified previous version by revision ID. The current version becomes the new active page, and the specified revision is restored as the live version. Returns the restored Page object. This operation creates a new revision entry in the page history. Use with caution as this overwrites the current page state.
